info.title: Mail Backend API
info.description: REST API for the Tauri desktop mail client — accounts, messages, drafts, folders.

tags.name: Client Config
tags.description: Safe runtime limits and capability values for the desktop client.

tags.name: Drafts
tags.description: Saving drafts into the Drafts folder (async, replaces semantics).

tags.name: Folders
tags.description: Account folder listing with folderRef and detected role.

tags.name: Mail Providers
tags.description: Predefined provider templates and e-mail-based auto-detection.

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: Real-time SSE notifications about sync progress and send outcomes.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Generic OAuth2 login flow. The client opens GET /api/v1/auth/oauth2/start?provider={google|microsoft|...} in the OS browser; the backend redirects to the provider, and once the user consents the provider calls /login/oauth2/code/{provider} (Spring Security filter), which completes the code-for-token exchange and redirects to /api/v1/auth/oauth2/success, where the account is saved or updated. The final page /auth-finished.html signals the end of the flow to the client.

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: CRUD over e-mail accounts — validation, password encryption, connection test.

tags.name: Messages — Actions
tags.description: Flag changes, move-to-trash, manual sync trigger.

tags.name: Messages — Write
tags.description: Send a message and prepare reply / forward drafts.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Local backend state for the desktop client

tags.name: Messages — Read
tags.description: Paginated folder listing, search, message detail, attachment download.

get.description: Returns one specific e-mail account by ID.

put.description: Replaces all account fields. Password/secret are re-encrypted if sent.

delete.description: Deletes the account and closes its IMAP connection (cleanup of the ImapConnectionManager pool).

get.description: Returns one contact by ID within the given account.

404.description: A contact with the given ID does not exist for the account (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND).
A contact with the given ID does not exist for the account (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND).

409.description: The target e-mail is already used by another contact (CONTACT_DUPLICATE).

delete.description: Removes the contact from the address book.

404.description: A contact with the given ID does not exist for the account (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND).

patch.description: Updates only the non-null fields from the request.

404.description: A contact with the given ID does not exist for the account (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND).

409.description: The target e-mail is already used by another contact (CONTACT_DUPLICATE).

post.description: Moves the message to a user-selected folder identified by folderRef from the folder list. The backend operation runs asynchronously; the local record is deleted immediately — after the target folder sync completes the message reappears with the same stableId contract.

204.description: Local record removed; the remote move runs asynchronously.

404.description: Message or target folder not found.

post.description: Starts an incremental sync of all folders of the given account. Returns 202 Accepted; the sync runs asynchronously.

202.description: Sync started; progress is delivered via the notification stream.

post.description: Asynchronously sends a message from the given account. Returns 202 Accepted with a sendId — the client tracks the outcome via the send_completed / send_failed notification stream event.

202.description: Send request accepted; outcome is delivered via the notification stream.

get.description: Returns a list of all registered e-mail accounts (including inactive ones).

post.description: Creates a new account, encrypts the password or OAuth secret and returns 201 with the complete DTO.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of drafts saved in the Drafts folder for the given account.

post.description: Asynchronously saves the draft into the Drafts folder. With the replaces={stableId} parameter, the old revision is deleted after a successful save. Returns 202 Accepted immediately — the draft's stableId becomes available after the next sync.

202.description: Draft save request accepted; processing continues asynchronously.

post.description: Asynchronously sends an existing draft over SMTP: the original MIME is fetched from the Drafts folder (preserving Message-ID, threading headers and attachments), sent, appended to Sent and then hard-deleted from Drafts. Returns 202 Accepted with a sendId — the client tracks the outcome via the send_completed / send_failed notification stream event.

202.description: Send request accepted; outcome is delivered via the notification stream.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of contacts. With the q parameter performs a case-insensitive substring search across email, name and surname. Optional `sort` (`name`/`surname`/`recent`) drives the order (default `surname`). Optional `label` (`WORK`/`HOME`/`OTHER`) filters to contacts with at least one e-mail bearing the given label.

schema.description: Label for the contact's email address. Optional — when the client omits it, null is stored (no label). Matching is case-insensitive, so both "home" and "HOME" pass.

post.description: Adds a new contact to the account's address book. Per-account e-mail uniqueness is enforced (409 on duplicate).

409.description: A contact with this e-mail already exists for the account (CONTACT_DUPLICATE).

post.description: Merges one or more source contacts into the target (all in one transaction). The target stays canonical (name/surname/primary are preserved); e-mails from the sources are added deduplicated by lowercase variant (collisions drop the source side); notes are concatenated. The source contacts are deleted. Limit: 9 sources per request, final e-mail count at most 10.

404.description: One of the source contacts or the target does not exist (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND).

post.summary: Add an e-mail address to a contact
post.description: Adds a new address without touching the primary flag of the others. The new e-mail only becomes primary if the contact had no address before.

409.description: The e-mail is already used by another contact or by this contact (CONTACT_DUPLICATE).

post.description: Creates up to 100 contacts in a single request. Each item has its own transaction — duplicates / validation errors do not affect the rest. The response is always 200 with per-item status (CREATED / FAILED).

delete.description: Deletes up to 100 contacts by ID. Non-existent IDs come back as FAILED / CONTACT_NOT_FOUND, the rest are deleted.

post.description: Verifies IMAP and SMTP connections for the given provider/custom config without saving the account.

patch.description: Sets or clears a flag on the message. Supported types: seen, flagged, answered (case-insensitive).
Marks the chosen address as primary; the other addresses of the contact have their primary flag cleared.

404.description: Contact or address does not exist (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND / RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND).

get.description: The desktop client uses this response at startup. Readiness means the API is ready to serve the UI; the first mail synchronization and accounts waiting for re-auth do not impact readiness.

Opens a Server-Sent Events stream. The server emits one of: `sync_completed` (after a folder sync produced new messages), `send_completed` (asynchronous SMTP send finished successfully), `send_failed` (asynchronous SMTP send failed; payload carries the AccountLastErrorCode in `errorCode`), or `thread_updated` (a conversation gained a message or two orphan chains merged). A heartbeat comment is emitted every 30 s so intermediaries do not close the connection.

200.description: Long-lived event stream. Each event has a `type` field that identifies the variant and matches the SSE event name.

get.description: Returns the full message detail including headers, recipients and attachment list (metadata, not content). For the message body see /content.

delete.description: Moves the message to the trash (soft delete). For permanent deletion the trash needs to be emptied.

get.description: Returns a pre-filled MailRequest for replying to the given message. Parameter all=true = reply-all (reply to every recipient).
Returns a pre-filled MailRequest for forwarding the message, including the quoted body and references to the original attachments.
Returns only the message body (HTML / plain text) without headers and metadata. Use after /detail to load the body lazily.

Streams the binary attachment content to the client. Content-Type is derived from the file name (fallback application/octet-stream); Content-Disposition contains both the ASCII and the UTF-8 (RFC 5987) form of the name.

200.description: Binary attachment payload streamed to the client.

get.description: Returns every message in the given conversation as a single ordered list. Ownership is enforced by the {accountId} path component — only threads owned by that account are reachable. The list is ordered by threadPosition ascending (matching receivedAt ascending). For per-message bodies fetch each member's /content endpoint separately.

404.description: No thread with that id is owned by the account.

get.description: Searches messages of an account across folders by the given query (subject/from/body). The query must not be empty and is capped at searchQueryMaxLength characters.
Returns a paginated list of messages (summary DTO) in the given folder by folderRef. The default size is taken from MailClientProperties; the cap is apiMaxPageSize.
Returns only safe limits and recommendations that the desktop client can use for UX validation. The backend remains the source of truth.

Target endpoint after a successful authorization at any provider. Spring Security redirects here after the flow completes. The controller hands the tokens to the service and sends the user to a static page the client captures. The client should not call this endpoint directly — the entry point is /api/v1/auth/oauth2/start?provider=...

Entry point for the client. Open it in the OS browser — the backend redirects to the selected provider, and after authorization completes the user lands on /auth-finished.html. On success the account is created (or updated) with authType=OAUTH2 and oauth2Provider matching the query parameter.
Returns folders with display name, folderRef and detected role (INBOX/SENT/TRASH/DRAFTS/JUNK/ARCHIVE/NEWSLETTERS/USER).
Returns all contacts of the account in vCard 4.0 format (RFC 6350) — text/vcard. Suitable for import into Apple Contacts, Google Contacts, Thunderbird and other clients.
Returns a flat list of addresses (contact x email) for typeahead. Ranking: prefix-email > prefix-surname > prefix-name > substring. Default limit 10, hard cap 20.
Returns the list of all predefined mail provider templates (IMAP/SMTP settings).
Returns one specific provider template by ID, or 404 if it does not exist.
Finds the matching provider template for the given e-mail address (by domain). Returns 404 if no template matches.

delete.summary: Delete an e-mail address from a contact
delete.description: If the primary address is being deleted, the first remaining one (by ID) is promoted. The last address cannot be deleted — a contact must have at least one.

400.description: Attempted to delete the last address of a contact (VALIDATION_ERROR).

404.description: Contact or address does not exist (CONTACT_NOT_FOUND / RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND).

ProblemDetail.description: RFC 9457 problem+json — format of all error responses.

type.description: URI identifier of the error type (e.g. .../errors/account_not_found).

title.description: Short human-readable description of the error class.

detail.description: Error detail intended for the user.

instance.description: URI of the specific occurrence (typically the request URI).

label.description: Address label (WORK/HOME/OTHER). Optional — send null or omit when no label.
Address label. null = the contact did not set one.

primary.description: Exactly one e-mail of a contact is primary (is_primary=1). Used for display and audit log.

folderRef.description: Opaque reference to the target folder obtained from the folder list.

sendId.description: Correlation id for tracking the async send outcome over the notification stream.

ready.description: True if the backend can serve user-facing API requests.

phase.description: Current phase of the readiness state machine.

stableId.description: Stable message identifier for the REST API. The client sends it to the detail, content and action endpoints.

threadId.description: Conversation identifier shared by every message of the same thread. Null only until the threading backfill has processed the message.

stableId.description: Stable message identifier for the REST API. The client uses it for message detail and message actions.

recipientsTo.description: Primary recipients (To). Useful for Drafts/Sent listings where the sender is the account owner.

threadId.description: Conversation identifier — every message of the same thread shares it. Null only until the threading backfill has processed the message (see /api/internal/threading/recompute). Frontends MAY group rows by this id; the V0.1.0 desktop client ignores it.
Stable thread identifier shared by every message of the conversation.

rootMessageId.description: RFC 5322 Message-ID of the oldest message in the thread. Null when the root message has no Message-ID.

participantsTotal.description: Number of messages in the thread.

unreadCount.description: Number of messages in the thread that are not yet marked as seen.

messages.description: Thread members in ascending threadPosition order.

ClientConfigResponse.description: Safe runtime limits and recommendations for the desktop client.

mailDefaultPageSize.description: Default page size for message listings.

mailApiMaxPageSize.description: Maximum allowed page size for paginated mail endpoints.

searchQueryMaxLength.description: Maximum length of the message full-text query.

contactQueryMaxLength.description: Maximum query length for contacts and autocomplete.

contactAutocompleteDefaultLimit.description: Default row limit for contact autocomplete.

contactAutocompleteMaxLimit.description: Maximum row limit for contact autocomplete.

attachmentMaxBytes.description: Recommended client-side limit for a single attachment in bytes.

attachmentTotalMaxBytes.description: Recommended client-side limit for all attachments in a single message in bytes.

largeAttachmentWarningBytes.description: Byte threshold above which the client warns about a large attachment.

folderRef.description: Opaque folder reference. The client does not parse it, only passes it back to other mail endpoints.

label.description: Label for the contact's email address. Optional — when the client omits it, null is stored (no label). Matching is case-insensitive, so both "home" and "HOME" pass.

ApiKeyAuth.description: Internal API key that the desktop client loads from session.json and sends in the X-API-KEY header.
ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-KEY
